genre_refs: James Baare Turnbull painting 'The Church Supper'
description: A straight reproduction of a mid-20th-century American regionalist/folk-style painting, 'The Church Supper' by James Baare Turnbull. It depicts an outdoor community potluck on a green churchyard lawn: dozens of townspeople in period clothing seated on benches at long white-clothed tables, with a red-brick church featuring a tall white steeple at upper left, leafy trees, a wrought-iron fence in the foreground, and other small-town buildings behind. The palette is warm and muted with a slightly naive, stylized perspective. There is no on-image text, caption, agency seal, or graphic overlay; it is shared as-is (caption appears only in the tweet). With no original design treatment it is not a 'meme' graphic. The Department of Labor uses it as wholesome Americana / faith-and-community devotional content (tweet: 'Happy Sunday, America... May God continue to shed His Grace on our Great Nation.'). Target audience: a patriotic, religiously inclined public.
